# UC-22.8.3 · SOC 2 Confidentiality Classification and DLP Event Audit

## Description

Audits Microsoft 365 DLP policy matches with actor, policy, and sensitive information types for confidentiality control testing and breach-readiness reporting.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes o365 and sourcetypes ms:o365:management. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Enable Office 365 Management Activity inputs in TA 4055 and confirm `Workload="Dlp"` events are ingested; (2) map `SensitiveInfoType` values to your data classification scheme via lookup `classification_tier.csv`; (3) alert on high-severity or high-volume exfil patterns; (4) retain per legal hold requirements; (5) optionally route to ES as correlation-search input. Indexes required: `index=o365`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ms:o365:management`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=o365` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (Workload, PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity, Operation).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Enable Office 365 Management Activity inputs in TA 4055 and confirm `Workload="Dlp"` events are ingested; (2) map `SensitiveInfoType` values to your data classification scheme via lookup `classification_tier.csv`; (3) alert on high-severity or high-volume exfil patterns; (4) retain per legal hold requirements; (5) optionally route to ES as correlation-search input.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload="Dlp"
| timechart span=1d count by Severity
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 Confidentiality Classification and DLP Event Audit (C1)** — Audits Microsoft 365 DLP policy matches with actor, policy, and sensitive information types for confidentiality control testing and breach-readiness reporting.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=o365` `sourcetype="ms:o365:management"` (Workload, PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity, Operation). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:management. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype="ms:o365:management". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by Severity** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (events by PolicyName), Heatmap (user x SensitiveInfoType), Line chart (daily volume by Severity), Table (sample evidence).

## SPL

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload="Dlp"
| stats count by PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity, Operation
| sort - count
| table PolicyName, UserPrincipalName, SensitiveInfoType, Severity, count, Operation
```
```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload="Dlp"
| timechart span=1d count by Severity
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (events by PolicyName), Heatmap (user x SensitiveInfoType), Line chart (daily volume by Severity), Table (sample evidence).
